data.title: SEC staff gives guidance on how securities laws could apply to crypto
data.description:
US Securities and Exchange Commission staff have given guidance on how federal securities laws could apply to crypto, saying companies issuing or dealing with tokens that could be securities should give better details about their business. The SEC’s Division of Corporation Finance said in a staff statement on April 10 that it was giving its views “to provide greater clarity on the application of the federal securities laws to crypto assets. ” The Division said its statement was made of observations of disclosures given in existing disclosure requirements and “addresses our views about certain specific disclosure questions that market participants have presented to the staff. ”The guidance, which the Division noted had “no legal force or effect,” said crypto companies who are giving disclosures about their business have typically shared a host of information about their operations, such as what the company specifically does, how any issued tokens work and how the business generates — or intends to generate — revenue
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/sec-guidance-how-federal-securities-laws-could-apply-crypto?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: SEC and Ripple reach preliminary settlement agreement, both parties apply to suspend appeal process
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to former U.S. federal prosecutor James K. Filan, the SEC and Ripple have jointly submitted a motion to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit to apply for an "abeyance" of the appeal and cross-appeal procedures of both parties, on the grounds that the two parties have reached a "principle settlement agreement" and are currently awaiting approval from the SEC Committee. If approved, they will apply to the local court for a confirmatory ruling. The court has canceled the original arrangement to submit the reply materials on April 16.

data.title: A whale unstaked more than 140,000 SOL and transferred them to Kraken
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to OnchainLens monitoring, a whale has released the pledge of 141,771 SOL (worth about $15.96 million) and sent it to Kraken. The whale still has 1.69 million SOL (worth about $191.02 million) pledged and is currently losing $22.67 million.

data.title: Spot gold rose to $3,217.97 per ounce, setting a new record high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Spot gold continued to rise, reaching a high of $3,217.97 per ounce, setting a new historical high, and has now fallen back to $3,206.10 per ounce.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Falls Below 100 for First Time Since July 2023
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) experienced a sharp decline of 60 points, dropping below the 100 mark. This marks the first time the index has fallen below this level since July 2023.

data.title: Significant Outflows in FBTC and FETH Continue for Third Day
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent monitoring by Farside indicates that FBTC experienced a net outflow of $74.6 million, while FETH saw a net outflow of $36 million. This marks the third consecutive trading day of net outflows for both FBTC and FETH.

data.title: US dollar index falls below 100 mark
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The US dollar index DXY plunged 60 points in the short term, breaking the 100 mark for the first time since July 2023. (Jinshi)

data.title: US SEC drops lawsuit against Helium developer Nova Labs
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Helium Network developer Nova Labs said the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has dropped charges that the company sold unregistered securities.
Helium said in a blog post on Thursday that this means that selling "hardware and distributing tokens to promote network growth" does not automatically make it a security. The SEC sued Nova Labs in January this year, and a few days later, former chairman Gary Gensler resigned on January 20. The lawsuit involves three Helium-related tokens - Helium Network Token (HNT), Helium Mobile Token (MOBILE) and Helium Internet of Things Network Token (IoT) - the SEC said that these tokens are unregistered securities.

data.title: Silk Road founder to make first public appearance at Las Vegas bitcoin conference
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Ross Ulbricht, the founder of the dark web Silk Road, who was pardoned by Trump, said he plans to make his first public appearance at an upcoming Bitcoin event. "This is official news. I am pleased to announce that I will make my first public appearance and give a speech at the Bitcoin conference in Las Vegas." In January of this year, President Trump signed an executive order to grant Ulbricht a full and unconditional pardon. The stories of Ulbricht and Silk Road are closely linked to the early history of Bitcoin. Ulbricht was sentenced to life imprisonment without parole for creating and operating the Silk Road. The Silk Road is a dark web market that specializes in the sale of drugs and other illegal products and services. The platform uses Bitcoin for transactions and has promoted the popularity of cryptocurrencies.

data.title: IRS DeFi Broker Rules Are Now Officially Repealed
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News US President Trump has signed a bill announcing the formal repeal of the IRS (Internal Revenue Service) DeFi crypto broker rules, the first cryptocurrency bill signed into law by the president. 
Previously, the 2024 rules classified DeFi platforms as brokers, meaning they had to file 1099 IRS forms for their users. Trump has said the rule "hinders American innovation" and "violates the privacy of ordinary Americans."
